title: Weekly Planning Session Frequency and Goal Achievement Rate Survey
description: This questionnaire measures how regularly a person conducts weekly planning sessions and how successfully they achieve the goals set during those sessions.

questions.text: How often do you hold a dedicated weekly planning session?

options.label: Every week without exception
Most weeks (3 out of 4)
Occasionally (1–2 times per month)
Rarely (a few times per year)
Never

questions.text: What proportion of the goals you set at the start of a week do you typically complete by the end of it?

options.label: Nearly all of them (90% or more)
Most of them (around 70–89%)
About half (50–69%)
Fewer than half (below 50%)
Rarely any (less than 20%)

questions.text: How long is your typical weekly planning session?

options.label: More than 30 minutes with structured thinking
Around 15–30 minutes
Under 10 minutes, mostly informal
I do not set aside dedicated planning time

questions.text: How do you document the goals you plan to achieve each week?

options.label: I write them in a dedicated planner or digital tool every week
I write them down most weeks
I occasionally write them down but not consistently
I do not record goals — I rely on memory or set none

questions.text: How clearly do you define each goal at the start of the week?

options.label: Always clearly, with specific steps and a target date
Mostly clear, though some details are missing
Usually vague — I know the goal but not the steps
I do not define goals in advance

questions.text: How consistently do you conduct an end-of-week review to assess which goals were achieved?

options.label: Every week as a fixed habit
Most weeks
Occasionally
Rarely
Never

questions.text: How well do you prioritise your weekly goals by importance and urgency?

options.label: Very well — I always rank tasks before starting the week
Fairly well — I prioritise most of the time
Inconsistently — I sometimes treat all tasks as equally important
I do not prioritise at all

questions.text: How realistic are the goals you typically set for a single week?

options.label: Always realistic — I consistently achieve what I plan
Mostly realistic — I occasionally overestimate
Sometimes unrealistic — goals frequently carry over
Usually too ambitious — most goals carry over to the next week
Always unrealistic — I rarely complete what I planned

questions.text: When you do not achieve a planned weekly goal, how do you typically respond?

options.label: I analyse what went wrong and adjust my approach for next week
I reschedule the goal with a revised plan
I carry it over without making any changes
I tend to drop the goal entirely

questions.text: How often do you break large weekly goals into smaller, actionable steps during planning?

options.label: Always — I map out sub-tasks for every major goal
Often — for most goals
Rarely — only for very complex goals
Never

questions.text: How often do unexpected events completely derail your weekly plan?

options.label: Rarely — I build buffer time and adapt well
Occasionally — perhaps once or twice a month
Very often — almost every week

questions.text: How consistent has your weekly planning habit been over the past three months?

options.label: Very consistent — I planned every single week
Mostly consistent — I missed only a few weeks
Inconsistent — I planned roughly half the weeks or fewer
I have not held a single planning session in the past three months

questions.text: How confident do you feel at the start of each week that you will achieve your planned goals?

options.label: Very confident — I trust my planning process
Moderately confident
Mostly doubtful
Not confident at all

questions.text: How often do you connect your weekly goals to longer-term personal or professional objectives?

options.label: Always — each weekly goal links to a broader plan
Sometimes
Never — my weekly goals are set independently of any longer plan

questions.text: How often do you postpone a planned weekly task even after scheduling it?

options.label: Almost never — I follow through on scheduled tasks
Occasionally
About half the time
Often
Almost always — tasks routinely carry over to the next week

questions.text: How promptly do you revise your weekly plan when circumstances change?

options.label: Immediately — I update the plan the same day
Usually within a day or two
Rarely — I usually continue with the original plan even if it no longer fits
I never adjust — I either complete the goals or abandon them

questions.text: How often do you reflect on what made your successfully completed goals achievable?

options.label: Regularly — I identify what worked and build on it
Occasionally
Never — I move on without reflection

questions.text: How effectively does the tool or system you use support your weekly planning?

options.label: Very effectively — it is structured, reliable, and easy to follow
Somewhat — it works but is often inconsistently used
I use no planning tool or system at all

questions.text: How often do you begin each week knowing clearly which goal is the single highest priority?

options.label: Always — I identify my top priority during planning
Usually
Sometimes
Never — I start the week without a clear priority

questions.text: Overall, how satisfied are you with your personal rate of weekly goal achievement?

options.label: Satisfied — I consistently achieve what I plan
Somewhat satisfied — achievement is hit or miss
Dissatisfied — I rarely achieve what I set out to do

states.label: Highly Effective Planner
states.summary: Excellent planning frequency with outstanding goal achievement.
states.description: Weekly sessions are held consistently, goals are clearly defined, and the vast majority are completed each week. No changes are needed; this level of self-regulation and commitment should be maintained and built upon.

states.label: Effective Planner
states.summary: Good planning consistency and solid goal achievement rate.
states.description: Planning sessions occur regularly and most goals are completed on time. Minor refinements to prioritisation, goal clarity, or end-of-week review habits could push performance to the highest level.

states.label: Moderate Planner
states.summary: Planning sessions occur but goal achievement is inconsistent.
states.description: Some weeks are productive while others fall short. Improving how goals are defined, breaking tasks into smaller steps, and conducting a brief weekly review would likely close the gap between planning and achievement.

states.label: Inconsistent Planner
states.summary: Planning sessions are irregular and fewer than half of goals are typically achieved.
states.description: Goal achievement suffers primarily because planning is unpredictable. Committing to a fixed weekly planning time, reducing the number of goals set each week, and introducing a simple accountability method are strongly advisable.

states.label: Low-Engagement Planner
states.summary: Planning rarely occurs and goal achievement is very low.
states.description: Without a reliable planning structure, most goals are left to chance. Establishing a simple, repeatable weekly routine, writing down at least one goal per week, and using a basic tracking tool would provide a meaningful starting point.

states.label: Disengaged Planner
states.summary: There is no structured weekly planning and goals are almost never achieved.
states.description: Significant support around self-regulation, goal-setting fundamentals, and time management is strongly recommended. Beginning with one small, clearly defined goal per week and reviewing it at the end of the week can help build initial momentum.
